feedback.text: My feedback on this Digital Fairness Act is that while I agree with the statement that NSFW/erotic art or media is harmful to children, a country's citizens ability to see that media if they wish too see it shouldn't be compromised if they are adults. They also shouldn't have to submit an ID or photo online as their identity is private and shouldn't need to be submitted to a website or social media app. If children really should be protected then have setting set up for that purpose so parents can have that setting on, it is a parent's job to protect their children from harmful things online and this act is just a way for them to do less work. I know child safety online is important but I just don't believe this is what this act is for, it's to give people and governments a chance to block media that they don't like due to personal bias, not moral obligations. If people want to see Erotic art or other forms of media they that is their right. Don't block it out because you don't like it. If you don't then just look away. You don't have to give attention if it bothers you, just block the artist if you don't like the Media that they post. Overall I do not support this act in Method, Practice or spirit. It is not to protect children it is an excuse to enforce censorship.
